title: Pyrus × mixta
taxonomy: Pyrus × mixta is a hybrid species of pear tree, resulting from a cross between Pyrus communis and Pyrus pyrifolia. It is often classified as a deciduous tree, belonging to the family Rosaceae. The Pyrus × mixta classification is further divided into several cultivars, each with unique characteristics. These cultivars are widely cultivated for their ornamental value and fruit production. Pyrus × mixta is commonly found in temperate climates and is valued for its attractive foliage and robust growth.

parts: Pyrus × mixta, commonly known as the ornamental pear, is a cross between Pyrus communis and Pyrus serotina. The bark, leaves, and fruits of Pyrus × mixta have been used in traditional medicine. The bark and leaves are known to have anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The fruits of the tree are used to treat various ailments, including fever, diarrhea, and respiratory issues. preparations: Pyrus × mixta, also known as the intermediate hawthorn, has been used in traditional herbal medicine for its potential health benefits. The plant's leaves, bark, and fruits are used to create various herbal preparations, which are believed to have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties. intro: Pyrus × mixta, also known as the mixopora pear or Chinese pear, is a hybrid plant species within the genus Pyrus, resulting from the cross-breeding of Pyrus bretschneideri and Pyrus communis. The fruit of the Pyrus × mixta plant has been used in traditional medicine for its health benefits, which include anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antimicrobial properties, and is believed to aid in digestion, reduce fever, and alleviate respiratory issues. The bioactive constituents of the Pyrus × mixta fruit include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and organic acids, which contribute to its medicinal properties. In traditional Chinese medicine, the Pyrus × mixta plant is commonly used to prepare teas, infusions, and juices, as well as being dried to create fruit extracts and powdered supplements.
